commit.message:
refactor: simplify to browser-based WASM sandbox

Remove all server-side infrastructure (API routes, auth, database,
server-side sandboxes, rate limiting, sessions). Replace with a
single HTML page that runs Pyodide and QuickJS in the browser.

Server is now ~20 lines: serves static files and proxies Claude API.
Agent loop, code execution, and UI all live in the browser.

Designed for MimiCloud and other platforms where no local CLI or
Python runtime is available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Story: Code Quality Audit & Technical Debt Cleanup (#177)

* fix: remove unused import and dead code from useProfileStore

- Remove unused legacyProfile field that was never implemented
- Clean up interface definition and store initialization

Co-authored-by: johnnyohwishingtree <johnnyohwishingtree@users.noreply.github.com>

- Update App.tsx to use @/ aliases for component and service imports
- Standardize all lazy-loaded screen imports in MainTabNavigator.tsx
- Convert RootNavigator.tsx imports to use @/ aliases
- Update Button.tsx and AccessibleButton.tsx to use @/ for utils

This improves consistency and maintainability across the codebase.

Co-authored-by: johnnyohwishingtree <johnnyohwishingtree@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: remove unused AccessibleButton component

- Remove AccessibleButton.tsx which was never used in the codebase
- Remove exports and type exports from ui/index.ts
- All screens use the standard Button component successfully
- Reduces bundle size and code complexity

Co-authored-by: johnnyohwishingtree <johnnyohwishingtree@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve critical linting issues

- Fix variable shadowing in useTripStore.ts (currentState vs state)
- Replace unused error variables with _error prefix in imageUtils.ts
- Apply ESLint auto-fixes for escape characters and formatting

Reduces linting errors and improves code quality compliance.

Co-authored-by: johnnyohwishingtree <johnnyohwishingtree@users.noreply.github.com>

* Auto-commit: save Claude's unpushed changes

Claude edited files but did not commit/push before the session ended.
This auto-commit preserves the work so it isn't lost.

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: revert private member accesses to bracket notation in tests

TypeScript errors were introduced when bracket notation was converted to dot notation.
Bracket notation bypasses visibility checks for private members in tests, while dot
notation enforces them. Reverted private member accesses back to bracket notation:

- PortalMonitor.performHealthChecks (5 instances in defensiveTesting.test.ts)
- ErrorTracker.deviceInfo and appState (2 instances in errorTracking.test.ts)
- PortalHealthChecker.healthHistory (1 instance in portalHealthChecker.test.ts)

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/code)

Co-authored-by: johnnyohwishingtree <johnnyohwishingtree@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: claude[bot] <209825114+claude[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: add IPICO reader control protocol implementation (#137)

## Summary
Implements full IPICO reader TCP control protocol (port 10000) with
bidirectional socket handling, request/response matching, and real-time
SSE events. Adds reader info polling (statistics, extended status,
date/time), clock synchronization, read mode control, and record
clearing. Includes comprehensive protocol documentation
reverse-engineered from pcap captures.

## Changes
- **ipico-core**: Control module with BCD encoding, LRC checksums,
command encoding, and response parsing (724 lines, 32 tests)
- **forwarder**: Reader control client, connection sequence, 30s status
polling, 6 HTTP API endpoints, SSE event broadcasts
- **forwarder-ui**: Reader info display, clock drift formatting,
expandable per-reader detail panel with 4 control buttons (Sync Clock,
Read Mode, Refresh, Clear Records)
- **docs**: Protocol specification with frame formats, encoding rules,
state diagrams, and 5 pcap captures demonstrating control sequences

## Testing
- cargo test -p ipico-core: 21 passed
- cargo test -p forwarder: all suites passed
- npm run check: 0 errors/warnings  
- npm run test: 26/26 passed
- cargo build --workspace: clean
- cargo build -p forwarder --features embed-ui: clean

## Notes
- Handles concurrent bidirectional control on shared reader TCP socket
via tokio::io::split
- Request/response matching with 2s timeout per command
- 3-step clear records sequence with CONFIG3 cycling per protocol
specification
- Clock drift calculation vs local time for UI display

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: Smart Auto-Import auto-creates folder from MyFin root

Previously, users had to manually pick a folder every time for auto-import.

Now:
- If MyFin root folder is already connected, auto-creates a Statements/
  folder inside the user's MyFin directory structure and sets it as the
  auto-import target — no manual folder pick needed
- On restore, auto-initializes from MyFin root if no saved handle exists
- Sub-folders for each bank/CC are auto-created inside Statements/
- Falls back to manual folder picker if no root folder is connected

commit.message:
Fix TLE loading stuck: reduce timeouts, add stale-cache fallback (#16)

Worst-case load time was ~14 min (8 layers × 8 retried fallbacks timing
out). Three changes:
- FETCH_TIMEOUT_DIRECT 8s→5s, FETCH_TIMEOUT_PROXY 12s→8s
- CORS proxy attempts 2→1 (no retry on last-resort fallbacks)
- Use stale localStorage cache when all network methods fail, instead of
  falling through to fully simulated orbits

Worst case is now ~4.5 min; users with any prior cached data load instantly.

https://claude.ai/code/session_012c7untW3xPNdBtjJ6QPTr4

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Infura budget: recalibrate for 3M/day (not month) — remove throttling

Infura free tier = 3M credits/DAY = 125k/hr. Our max projected usage
(all 5 chains at full block rate) is ~2,820 calls/hr = 2.3% of budget.

- HOURLY_CAP: 3,000 → 100,000 (safety net, not a throttle)
- CHAIN_MIN_INTERVAL: 30s → 2s (just blocks duplicate events, not real limiting)
- Arbitrum/Avalanche poll interval: back to 15s (was 30s due to old min interval)

All chains now run at natural block frequency. Override via env vars if needed.

Nouveau HUB Gestion :
- [💃 Contenus] = Cours + Offres + Audio Upload inline + Master Control Audio
- [🎬 Vidéo Hero] = 3 media slots + Master Control Vidéos
- [🖼️ Ma Vitrine] = Branding, Couleurs, Logo/Favicon, Liens, Affiche, SEO, FAQ, Description

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add logout button + session expiry detection to admin panel

- Logout button in header that destroys session and redirects to login
- Auth status indicator (green = logged in, red = expired)
- check_auth endpoint for session validation
- Auto-check every 2 minutes + on page load
- All API calls detect 401 and redirect to login automatically

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add pyproject.toml with pinned dependencies and dev extras

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

add ADR-008: multi-arch container image and CD pipeline

ADR-008 — Containerization and deployment:
- OCI container image as the deployment artifact (no vendor lock-in)
- Two-stage Dockerfile: node:20-alpine build → nginx:1.27-alpine serve
- Multi-architecture: linux/amd64 + linux/arm64 via Docker Buildx + QEMU
- Registry: GitHub Container Registry (ghcr.io), authenticated via GITHUB_TOKEN
- Tagging: sha-<git-sha> (immutable) + latest + semver on version tags
- Image size gate: hard failure if compressed image exceeds 30 MB
- nginx.conf spec: SPA routing, security headers, gzip, cache policy
- Two workflow files: ci.yml (test only) and release.yml (test + publish)
- Rejected: static host (vendor lock-in), amd64-only (ARM64 cost), Caddy (HTTPS handled by infra layer)

ADR-007 update:
- Clarified ci.yml scope as test-only, references ADR-008 for CD pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
